cognates.correspondence_path: Ar ʔ ↔ Am ʔ; Ar n ↔ Am n; Ar f ↔ Am f. Regular reflexes of *ʔnp.
cognates.notes: Amharic shows metathesis of the nasal/labial components: *ʔanp- vs Am ʔafn-.

cognates.correspondence_path: He ʔ ↔ Am ʔ; He p ↔ Am f. Regular reflexes of *ʔap-.
cognates.notes: Hebrew simplifies the root to a biliteral base (C1-C2).

caveats: v0 prototype: this is an LLM-generated analysis, not a lexicon lookup. Results may be incomplete or incorrect, especially for Syriac, Tigrinya, and rare forms. Verify against Wiktionary or a scholarly lexicon before citing.
The Amharic root extraction is speculative regarding the historical development of the 'ጫ' suffix, which might be a later denominal formation.
The root corresponds to Proto-Semitic *ʔ-n-p, but many Semitic languages shift the *p to *f.
